To: Chair Simmens  and  Members  of the Public Works Commission Prepared  by: Troy Spayd, Assistant Public  Works Director/City Engineer Approved  by: Rob  DuBoux,  Public Works Director/City Engineer Date prepared: August 20, 2021 Meeting  date: August 25, 2021 Subject: Capital  Improvement  Projects and  Disaster Recovery  Projects Status Report

Civic Center Way Improvements

Civic Center  Way Improvements ¾ Updates:

  The  contractor  continues  to  maintain  the  new  planting  material during the establishment  period per the contract documents. This period ends at the end  of August 2021.

0de83df8-5d77-4c24-b016-1e788ee52c91.label: text_chunk

Staff will be  closing out the project.

Malibu Park Drainage Improvements

¾ Project  Description:

  This  project  consists  of  storm  drain  repairs  within  the Malibu  Park  neighborhood.  These  repairs  are  the  result of  damage  caused by the  subsequent  storms after the  Woolsey  Fire.

7793575d-73b4-436d-bb3a-581bb906bf90.label: text_chunk
7793575d-73b4-436d-bb3a-581bb906bf90.text: Work will include  clearing and  grubbing,  demolition,  cold  milling, removal  of  existing corrugated  metal pipe, new asphalt berms/dikes, pavement  repairs, pavement  overlay, striping, new  reinforced  concrete pipes, and  rock slope  protection.

f79b0741-88f5-4bee-a413-ca6a4e764c7b.label: text_chunk
f79b0741-88f5-4bee-a413-ca6a4e764c7b.text:
¾ Updates:

  Construction  started  at  the  Morning  View  location  and  included installing  a  new  60"  HDPE  pipe  and  constructing  a  new  inlet  basin  and headwall.  The contractor will also begin working at the other locations per the project  plans  and  specifications.  Work  will  include  temporary  lane  closures with flaggers.

65778e4d-be08-455f-96b0-cf45260c0bfe.label: text_chunk
65778e4d-be08-455f-96b0-cf45260c0bfe.text:
¾ Project Schedule:  August 2021  through  October 2021

2021 Annual Street Maintenance

2021  Annual  Street Maintenance ¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  provides  for  the  reconstruction  and maintenance  of  City  streets.  Including  asphalt  pavement  repairs,  Type  II slurry seal, speed  humps  and new  thermoplastic  striping.

Vehicle Protection Devices

¾ Project  Description:

   Per  Malibu  Municipal  Code  Section  17.48.070  requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within  the  City’s right-of-way  and  City-owned  property  that  would  require  a vehicle impact protection  device.

¾ Project Description:

   This project consists of design and  construction of a few storm drains on Clover  Heights.  The existing storm drain  facility ends  at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.

e932cacc-a936-4979-8ddc-d8097f2acaa4.text: During  storms,  this intersection  floods  and  causes  debris to block the  road.   An  extended  storm drain towards  the end  of Clover Heights will help  eliminate  this issue.

properties.entity_description: Corrugated metal pipe is a type of pipe made from metal that has a wavy or ridged surface, commonly used for drainage and water management.
Asphalt berms/dikes are structures made from asphalt used to manage water flow and provide support in construction projects.
Reinforced concrete pipes are strong, durable pipes made from concrete and reinforced with steel, used for various drainage and sewage applications.
Pavement refers to the durable surface material used for roads, parking lots, and other surfaces, typically made from asphalt or concrete.

properties.relationship_description: The work includes the removal of existing corrugated metal pipes as part of the construction and maintenance activities.
The contractor is responsible for executing the construction work according to the project plans and specifications.

properties.entity_description: A 60-inch High-Density Polyethylene (HDPE) pipe is a type of plastic pipe used for various construction and drainage applications, known for its durability and resistance to corrosion.
An inlet basin is a structure designed to collect and manage stormwater runoff, allowing it to flow into a drainage system.
A headwall is a structure at the end of a drainage pipe that helps to control water flow and prevent erosion.

properties.relationship_description: The construction at the Morning View location includes the installation of a 60-inch HDPE pipe as part of the infrastructure development.

community_summary.28:
The relationships outlined highlight the impact of the Woolsey Fire on various infrastructure projects aimed at repair and improvement. 

1. **Guardrail Replacement Citywide**: This project was initiated due to damage caused by the Woolsey Fire, which affected guardrails throughout the city. The fire's destruction necessitated repairs to ensure safety and functionality.

2. **Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs**: The Woolsey Fire also caused significant damage to storm drain systems, prompting the Latigo Canyon Road Culvert Repairs project to address these issues.

3. **Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements**: Similarly, the fire resulted in damage to storm drain facilities and roadway embankments, leading to the need for the Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements project.

Overall, the Woolsey Fire has been a catalyst for multiple infrastructure repair initiatives, specifically targeting damaged guardrails and storm drain systems across the affected areas.
community_summary.29: The relationships involve two key entities: **Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs** and **Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs**. Both projects are associated with FEMA and focus on infrastructure repairs in the Corral Canyon area. However, it is important to note that while the Corral Canyon Road Bridge Repairs project was completed, the Corral Canyon Culvert Repairs project was cancelled. This highlights a significant distinction in the status of these related projects within the context of infrastructure development and disaster recovery efforts.
community_summary.30: The Clover Heights Storm Drain project is significantly supported and funded by the Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA). This relationship underscores the project's importance in disaster management and infrastructure development, highlighting FEMA's role in providing financial assistance for such initiatives.
community_summary.31: The relationship between Clover Heights and the Intersection highlights the significant impact of flooding issues in Clover Heights on the Intersection. Specifically, the flooding leads to debris accumulation, which obstructs the road at this location. This relationship underscores the interconnectedness of environmental conditions and infrastructure, illustrating how local flooding can disrupt traffic and accessibility at critical junctions.
community_summary.32:
**Summary of Relationships Involving the City:**

1. **Disaster Recovery Projects**: The City initiates Disaster Recovery Projects aimed at restoring and rebuilding services and infrastructure following disasters, highlighting its proactive role in emergency management.

2. **Funding from Measure W**: The City has secured funding from Measure W, establishing a financial relationship that supports its projects, particularly in disaster recovery.

3. **Consultant Engagement**: The City actively seeks proposals from consultants for construction management services, indicating its reliance on external expertise for project execution. This includes a formal request for proposals specifically targeting consultants.

4. **Planning Commission Approval**: The City obtained a project Coastal Development Permit (CDP) from the Planning Commission, signifying adherence to regulatory processes and formal approval for its projects.

5. **State Funding Application**: The City applied to the State for the State Revolving Fund program, demonstrating its initiative to secure additional funding for infrastructure projects.

6. **Vehicle Impact Protection**: The City enforces regulations regarding the installation of vehicle impact protection devices, ensuring compliance with municipal codes to enhance public safety.

7. **Storm Drain Management**: The City manages and maintains storm drains, which is a critical aspect of its infrastructure services, ensuring effective drainage and flood prevention.

8. **Infrastructure Project Proposals**: The City has issued requests for qualifications and proposals from qualified firms to undertake various infrastructure-related projects, reflecting its commitment to improving community services through collaboration with specialized firms.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City's multifaceted approach to infrastructure management, disaster recovery, regulatory compliance, and community safety, emphasizing its role as a proactive and responsible entity in urban governance.
community_summary.33: The California Office of Emergency Services (CalOES) is overseeing the Birdview Avenue Improvements project. This involvement indicates that the project is a state initiative requiring coordination and oversight from CalOES, highlighting the agency's role in ensuring the project's alignment with state regulations and emergency management protocols.

Overall, the Staff plays a crucial role in both project planning and communication with Properties, ensuring that necessary information is conveyed effectively for the public bidding process.

The summary of the relationships is as follows:

1. **Planning Commission and Westward Beach Road Improvements Project**: The Planning Commission approved the design plans for the Westward Beach Road Improvements Project on September 20, 2021. This indicates a significant endorsement of the project by the Commission.

The relationships highlight the involvement of FEMA in funding and supporting infrastructure projects aimed at disaster recovery and improvements. 

1. **Trancas Canyon Park Planting and Irrigation Repairs**: This project is associated with FEMA, which is likely providing funding or support due to its focus on disaster recovery and infrastructure enhancements.

The relationships involving the City highlight its multifaceted role in managing public infrastructure and responding to community needs. 

1. **Capital Improvements Projects**: The City manages these projects to enhance public infrastructure, ensuring that community facilities and services are improved and maintained.

2. **Disaster Recovery Projects**: The City initiates these projects to restore services and infrastructure following disasters, demonstrating its commitment to community resilience and recovery.

3. **Vehicle Protection Devices**: The City enforces regulations for the installation of vehicle protection devices, reflecting its responsibility for public safety as outlined in the Malibu Municipal Code.

4. **FEMA Grant**: The City has been awarded a grant from FEMA, establishing a financial relationship aimed at funding hazard mitigation efforts, which underscores the importance of federal support in local disaster preparedness.

5. **Contract with Acoustic Technology**: The City has contracted Acoustic Technology to design outdoor warning sirens, indicating a professional collaboration focused on enhancing emergency communication systems.

6. **Bidding Process**: On April 7, 2022, the City received bids, showcasing its active role in procurement and project management.

7. **Agreement Award**: The City awarded an agreement on June 13, further illustrating its involvement in formalizing contracts and partnerships for various projects.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City's proactive approach to infrastructure management, disaster recovery, public safety, and collaboration with external entities for community enhancement.
